name: Hike of Bagnères-de-Bigorres
region_intro: Hiking around Bagnères-De-Bigorre offers access to diverse landscapes within the Hautes-Pyrénées department of France. The region is characterized by its extensive network of trails, leading through lush valleys, dense forests, and towards numerous glacial lakes. Hikers can explore a variety of terrain, from serene lakeside paths to challenging mountain passes and peaks, providing varied elevation gains. This area serves as a gateway to the Pyrenees, featuring prominent natural attractions and a wide range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Bagnères-De-Bigorre The most popular hiking route is The Néouvielle lakes via Col d'Estoudou, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the protected Néouvielle natural reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Aubert and Aumar lakes, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This trail provides views of these adjoining lakes and the surrounding peaks. Local hikers also love the Lac de l'Oule Tour, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through mountain scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Bagnères-De-Bigorre is defined by picturesque glacial lakes, challenging mountain passes, and lush valleys. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to demanding treks. The routes in Bagnères-De-Bigorre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4800 reviews. More than 51000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bagnères-De-Bigorre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: Bagnères-De-Bigorre offers an extensive network of trails, with over 900 hiking routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain treks, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: The region features diverse terrain, from lush valleys and dense forests to serene glacial lakes and challenging mountain passes. You'll encounter varied elevation gains, with paths ranging from well-maintained trails to more rugged, alpine environments.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: Yes, Bagnères-De-Bigorre has over 200 easy routes suitable for families. For a gentle walk, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877595887/the-tour-of-lake-loudenvielle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;the tour of lake Loudenvielle&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 5 km long and offers a pleasant experience around the lake.

Q: Can I find challenging multi-day hikes around Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: While the guide focuses on day hikes, the region is known for its demanding treks. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422215906/campbieil-peak" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campbieil peak&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383204039/neouvielle-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle Tour&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and longer durations, providing a taste of more strenuous mountain experiences. The area is a gateway to the Pyrenees, offering options for those seeking a challenge.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore numerous glacial lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Oule&lt;/a&gt;, and the Néouvielle Lakes. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aspin&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning views, and the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5448276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic du Midi de Bigorre&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark visible from many trails.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in Bagnères-De-Bigorre are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383170919/lac-de-l-oule-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Oule Tour&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail. The region's extensive network of trails often allows for loop options, especially around its many lakes and valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: The best time for hiking in Bagnères-De-Bigorre is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is typically mild, and mountain passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions to explore the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: Many trails in the Bagnères-De-Bigorre area are dog-friendly, especially those outside of strict nature reserves. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Specific regulations may apply in certain areas like the Néouvielle Natural Reserve, so check local signage.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on hikes in Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: Scenic viewpoints are abundant throughout the region. Peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5557351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campbieil Peak&lt;/a&gt; and passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1036161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hourquette d’Aubert&lt;/a&gt; offer expansive panoramas of the Pyrenees. Many routes around the glacial lakes also provide stunning vistas of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While some trailheads might be accessible by local bus services, especially during peak tourist season, public transport options can be limited for more remote starting points. It's often recommended to check local bus schedules in Bagnères-De-Bigorre or consider carpooling for greater flexibility in reaching various trailheads.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bagnères-De-Bigorre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4800 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning variety of landscapes, from serene lakes to challenging mountain passes, and the extensive network of well-maintained trails.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature multiple lakes?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its glacial lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383151171/the-neouvielle-lakes-via-col-d-estoudou" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Néouvielle lakes via Col d'Estoudou&lt;/a&gt; route takes you through a protected natural reserve dotted with several lakes. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383198270/aubert-and-aumar-lakes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aubert and Aumar lakes&lt;/a&gt; trail, offering superb views of these adjoining bodies of water.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in this area?
A: Hike durations vary significantly based on difficulty and length. Easy routes, like the tour of Lake Loudenvielle, can be completed in under 2 hours. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383170919/lac-de-l-oule-tour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de l'Oule Tour&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 3-4 hours. More difficult treks, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422223923/bastan-lakes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastan lakes&lt;/a&gt;, can extend to 6-7 hours or more.
